extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a can only be set true by r2 (priority 50), which requires hepatic_impairment AND g6pd_deficiency AND immunosuppressed AND qt_prolongation all true; otherwise it falls to the default false. rec_drug_b is governed by two competing rules: r3 (priority 50) sets it true when hepatic_impairment is true, and r4 (priority 90) sets it false under the exact same four-way conjunction that triggers r2. The only way to make rec_drug_a true is to satisfy all four conditions — but that identical condition fires r4, and since r4's priority (90) exceeds r3's (50), r4 wins the conflict on rec_drug_b, forcing rec_drug_b = false. Therefore in every input where rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is necessarily false; and when the four-way conjunction is not met, rec_drug_a stays false. The conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is unsatisfiable across the entire 2^5 input space. Property holds.
